item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I originally skipped out on this one. Then SunnyClockwork made a picture of it and I decided to check it out. Was not disappointed and gave me a good laugh. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They had us in the first half, not gonna lie.

I love how, as I'm sitting there trying to puzzle out just how long boi fish god relates to the staff room microwave

between 37,000 and 39,000 kilometers

this is what made me stop and point at the screen in disbelief.

Like, come on, what??

Anyway, praise the fish. +1

item.title: Help I'm dumb
item.content:encoded: Is it a regular fish or not?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could have sworn i commented earlier so I'll just comment again.

Praise the fish.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. It's one of those 'its obviously not real … unless?'
fun mindgames. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1, except for this typo I found:

The fish is referred to as a long boy. It's spelled "long boi."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ah-hem…

praise length fish

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oops ignore this

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
That is one long boy
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Worlds collide…..,.,, long friends….,...,.. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Long boy!!! Would Long Boy like to meet a similarly long friend? I want them to meet so badly. Maybe we can kinda move some of the underwater lengths next to each other and let them touch.

Regardless, this is… very accurate to my understanding of human psychology, speaking as someone who accidentally started a cult centered around her plushie. This is me, working in the Foundation. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm entertained, yet a little let down. Not punchline-ey enough to be a -J but not serious enough to be a main list. And being a main list SCP I wasn't hoping for a punchline. I was honestly hoping for it to take a turn towards horror near the end, but it never did. Like something light hearted and funny that is some horribly thing underneath.
Better than I could ever do writing an SCP, and an pretty damn good first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote.

I'd say, very oddly, that, I like it, maybe just because it's not a -J.

I mean, not only you didn't put it in -J, but also it's not funny or, no offense, "shallow" enough to be a -J. Thus I would laugh at first, and think back later, realizing the church is probably rather a serious thing than a joke. That's a very subtle line.

However, such literary effect is based on the whole atmosphere of the website and my being familiar with those skips and -Js. The article itself may be not strong enough. So no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of SCPs are funny, but this one doesn't just make me laugh, it makes me happy. This, the longest of boys, is legitimately delightful. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Long fish cult best fish cult. Made me laugh! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to say that I really enjoyed this one. I've been conditioned by enough infohazards on this site that when I read the security logs, I genuinely believed the point you were making was that the long boy had infected these people so deeply that the People's Church of the Fish That Just Goes On Forever seemed like a perfectly normal and reasonable thing, so much so that its members would joke about it the same way religious people often do about their real beliefs. Like, it would not be out of place for a Christian with a certain type of humour to joke about sacrificing their child for RC Cola, in my experience, and I thought that was what you were going for. So the reveal that it was actually just a joke really landed for me. +1

Edit: I forgot to say that while I see what you're doing with the wizard figure line, I don't really think you need it and I'd have enjoyed the skip slightly more if it was cut. But that's a minor thing, I still love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"neither long nor a boy" +1

(also i was +69. nice)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i've heard of "toeing the line of a -J" but "toeing the line of LOLFoundation" is… also not exactly new to me.

but good job making the foundation feel just a little more human on this one. i think anyone in a medium-stress office environment can relate to this one. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me likey. there are tons of IRL examples of people in high importance jobs (eg. working in major computer servers) with similar superstitions, so it's nice to see an equivalent to that in the foundation.

Praise our lengthy overlord, +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it. Enough ambiguity to be scary, enough silliness to be funny, enough mystery waiting to be uncovered for it to be something you might ponder about. (+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, it's a fun SCP

Especially the twist that the whole thing is… not a memetic effect but just a joke formed by the entire research team.

Though as Genshed notes: is it?

Also:

Praise L O N G F I S H!
yeah uhh I believe I said "what the fuck radd" in the irc chat and that still stands absolutely amazing but still just????? what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This goes up to the line between mainlist and -J, then dances the tarantella all *over* that line.

I'm half convinced that there really is a covert fish cult, and the alleged explanation is a bluff.

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: I admittedly did struggle with that. I think I'll give this a day or so to sit and I'll come back to it to see if I can't refine it a little more. Thanks for your notes!

I'm always down for an SCP which shows the people writing it being affected by the SCP, I wrote something similar myself recently. But to work, these pieces require some level of subtlety and subterfuge to lead the reader along so that revelation has some impact and meaning.

This gives up the game far too early, the reader knows what's going on the entire time and that sucks any punch or horror the fish church could have brought to the table. When the reader knows what's going on the entire time there's no tension or emotional impact. It can be amusing, but nothing more than that.

I did like the e-mail signature line, keep that if and when you make revisions to this in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You did a really good job having fun with a goofy little idea like this. Very well executed and I look forward to reading more of your work

+1
The biggest longest boi who we hope to find the tail or the head of praise the long big boi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Praise be the boy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Boy so long
Long long boy
Boy so long it could form a religion

item.title: Boomers just don't understand
item.content:encoded: I absolutely adore the characters here, and the long boi itself is just a lovely thing. Good work! +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hullo, it is my first SCP \ (•◡•) / I am finally giving it a try
Special thanks to Uncle Nicolini, MalyceGraves, Rounderhouse, Crashington, Mew-ltiverse and Lord_of_Laugh for their help! You guys are invaluable.

Also thanks to Nico and Rounder for letting me use their cool email format, I appreciate you. (ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:･ﾟ✧
